But shall we say, 'Of human origin'?"--they were afraid of the crowd, for all regarded John as truly a prophet.
So they answered Jesus, "We do not know." And Jesus said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things."

translations.DBH: Jesus replied and said to him, "You have faith because I told you I saw you below the fig tree? You shall see greater things than these."
translations.NRSVUE: Jesus answered, "Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these."
translations.LAMSA: Jesus said to him, Do you believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You shall see greater things than these.

translations.DBH: And he says to him, "Amen, amen, I tell you, you shall see the heavens open and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man."
translations.NRSVUE: And he said to him, "Very truly, I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man."
translations.LAMSA: He said to him, Truly, truly, I say to all of you that from now on you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending to the Son of man.

translations.DBH: In reply Jesus said to him, "Amen, amen, I tell you, unless someone is born from above, he cannot see the Kingdom of God."
translations.NRSVUE: Jesus answered him, "Very truly, I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God without being born from above."
translations.LAMSA: Jesus answered, saying to him, Truly, truly, I say to you, If a man is not born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.

translations.DBH: Nicodemus says to him, "How can a man be born when he is old? He is not able to enter his mother's womb a second time and then be born?"
translations.NRSVUE: Nicodemus said to him, "How can anyone be born after having grown old? Can one enter a second time into the mother's womb and be born?"
translations.LAMSA: Nicodemus said to him, How can an old man be born again? Can he enter again a second time into his mother's womb and be born?

translations.DBH: Jesus replied, "Amen, amen, I tell you, unless a man is born of water and spirit, he cannot enter into the Kingdom of God.
translations.NRSVUE: Jesus answered, "Very truly, I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God without being born of water and Spirit.
translations.LAMSA: Jesus answered, saying to him, Truly, truly, I say to you, If a man is not born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.

translations.DBH: He spoke these words while teaching in the far more scandalous than commendable in most eyes for Jesus to have allowed an adulteress to go away not only unpunished, but entirely without rebuke. For another, there is good reason to think the episode may in fact be drawn from an older narrative source than the Gospel itself: there is a tale of a very sinful woman that the early second-century Christian Papias mentioned as being part of the lost Gospel of the Hebrews; the Syrian Didascalia (from the third century) cites "the story of the adulteress"; the Constitutions of the Apostles (in a portion probably also from the third century) relates a similar story of a sinful woman whom Jesus refused to condemn; and both Didymus the Blind and Jerome mention the tale as appearing in many manuscripts before the end of the fourth century. Moreover, the earliest texts of John do not merely lack the story; in its place are diacritical marks indicating that something (maybe the same story, maybe something else) has been omitted. Augustine, in fact, aware of the story's absence from many texts of the Gospel, opined that perhaps it had been removed because of the offense it might give to pious souls unable to understand how Christ could excuse so grave a transgression with no more than an exhortation to sin no more. It seems that the story was something of a freely floating tradition, perhaps with very deep roots in Christian memory, one that was not originally firmly associated with any particular Gospel text, but that was inserted in various versions of Luke or John because it was too beautiful and too illuminating of Christ's ministry and person to be left out of the church's lectionary cycle (and hence out of scripture). treasury of the Temple; still no one seized him, because his hour had not yet come.

translations.NRSVUE: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
translations.LAMSA: In that day you will know that I am with my Father, and you are with me, and I am with you.

translations.DBH: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them, that one is the one who loves me, and whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him and will manifest myself to him."
translations.NRSVUE: They who have my commandments and keep them are those who love me, and those who love me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them."
translations.LAMSA: He who has my commandments with him and obeys them is the one who loves me; he who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him and reveal myself to him.

translations.DBH: Judas--not Iscariot--says to him, "Lord, what has happened then, that you are about to manifest yourself to us and not to the cosmos?"
translations.NRSVUE: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, "Lord, how is it that you will reveal yourself to us and not to the world?"
translations.LAMSA: Judah (not of Iscariot) said to him, My Lord, why is it that you will reveal yourself to us and not to the world?

translations.DBH: Jesus answered and said to him, "If someone loves me, he will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and will make our home with him.
translations.NRSVUE: Jesus answered him, "Those who love me will keep my word, and my Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
translations.LAMSA: Jesus answered, saying to him, He who loves me keeps my word; and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make a place of abode with him.

translations.DBH: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words; and the word that you hear is not mine but rather that of the Father who has sent me.
translations.NRSVUE: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words, and the word that you hear is not mine but is from the Father who sent me.
translations.LAMSA: But he who does not love me does not keep my word; and this word which you hear is not my own but the Father's who sent me.

translations.DBH: These things I have spoken to you while remaining with you;
translations.NRSVUE: "I have said these things to you while I am still with you.
translations.LAMSA: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.

translations.DBH: but the advocate, the Spirit, the Holy One, which the Father will send in my name, that one will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I have told you.
translations.NRSVUE: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and remind you of all that I have said to you.
translations.LAMSA: But the Comforter, the Holy Spirit, whom my Father will send in my name will teach you everything, and remind you of everything which I tell you.

translations.DBH: Peace I leave you, my peace I give to you; I give to you not as the cosmos gives. Do not let your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
translations.NRSVUE: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled, and do not let them be afraid.
translations.LAMSA: Peace I leave with you; my own peace I give you; not as the world gives, I give to you. Let not your heart be troubled and do not be afraid.

translations.DBH: You heard that I told you that I am going away and am coming to you. If you had loved me you would have rejoiced that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I.
translations.NRSVUE: You heard me say to you, 'I am going away, and I am coming to you.' If you loved me, you would rejoice that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I.
translations.LAMSA: You heard that I told you, I am going away, and I will come to you. If you loved me, you would rejoice because I am going to my Father; for my Father is greater than I.

translations.DBH: And now I have told you before it happens, so that when it happens you might have faith.
translations.NRSVUE: And now I have told you this before it occurs, so that when it does occur you may believe.
translations.LAMSA: And now behold, I have told you before it happens, so that when it does happen, you may believe.

translations.DBH: I will no longer speak much with you, for the Archon of the cosmos is coming--and he has no hold in me--31but so that the cosmos may know that I love the Father, and that just as the Father has commanded me so I do. Arise, let us go from here.
translations.NRSVUE: I will no longer talk much with you, for the ruler of this world is coming. He has no power over me,
translations.LAMSA: Hereafter I will not talk much with you; for the prince of this world comes; and yet he has nothing against me.

translations.NRSVUE: but I do as the Father has commanded me, so that the world may know that I love the Father. Rise, let us be on our way.
translations.LAMSA: But that the world may know that I love my Father, and as my Father has commanded me, so I do. Arise, let us go away from here.

translations.DBH: "I am the true vine and my Father is the husbandman.
translations.NRSVUE: "I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinegrower.
translations.LAMSA: I AM the true vine, and my Father is the laborer.

translations.DBH: Every branch in me that does not bear fruit he takes away; and every branch that bears fruit he trims clean so that it might bear more fruit.
translations.NRSVUE: He removes every branch in me that bears no fruit. Every branch that bears fruit he prunes to make it bear more fruit.
translations.LAMSA: Every branch in me that does not bear fruit, he cuts off; and the one which bears fruit, he prunes so that it may bring forth more fruit.

translations.DBH: You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you.
translations.NRSVUE: You have already been cleansed by the word that I have spoken to you.
translations.LAMSA: You have already been pruned because of the word which I have spoken to you.

translations.DBH: Remain in me--and I in you. Just as the branch cannot bear fruit from itself unless it remain in the vine, so neither can you unless you remain in me.
translations.NRSVUE: Abide in me as I abide in you. Just as the branch cannot bear fruit by itself unless it abides in the vine, neither can you unless you abide in me.
translations.LAMSA: Remain with me and I with you. Just as a branch cannot give fruit by itself unless it remains in the vine, even so you cannot unless you remain with me.

translations.DBH: I am the vine, you are the branches; the one remaining in me and I in him, this one bears plentiful fruit, because apart from me you can do nothing.
translations.NRSVUE: I am the vine; you are the branches. Those who abide in me and I in them bear much fruit, because apart from me you can do nothing.
translations.LAMSA: I am the vine, you are the branches. He who remains with me, and I with him, will bear abundant fruit; for without me you can do nothing.

translations.DBH: Unless someone remain in me he is like the branch that has been cast outside and has withered, and they gather them up and cast them in the fire, and they are burned.
translations.NRSVUE: Whoever does not abide in me is thrown away like a branch and withers; such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
translations.LAMSA: Unless a man remains with me, he will be cast outside like a branch which is withered, which they pick up and throw into the fire to be burned.

translations.DBH: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask whatever you wish, and it shall happen for you.
translations.NRSVUE: If you abide in me and my words abide in you, ask for whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
translations.LAMSA: If you remain with me, and my words remain with you, whatever you ask shall be done for you.

translations.DBH: By this my Father has been glorified: that you bear plentiful fruit and will become my disciples.
translations.NRSVUE: My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit and become my disciples.
translations.LAMSA: In this the Father will be glorified, that you bear abundant fruit and be my disciples.

translations.DBH: As the Father has loved me I have also loved you; remain in my love.
translations.NRSVUE: As the Father has loved me, so I have loved you; abide in my love.
translations.LAMSA: Just as my Father has loved me, I also have loved you; abide in my love.

translations.DBH: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I have kept my Father's commandments and remain in his love.
translations.NRSVUE: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father's commandments and abide in his love.
translations.LAMSA: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, even as I have kept my Father's commandments and abide in his love.

translations.DBH: I have spoken these things to you that my joy may be in you and your joy may be made full.
translations.NRSVUE: I have said these things to you so that my joy may be in you and that your joy may be complete.
translations.LAMSA: I have spoken these things to you that my joy may be in you and that your joy may be full.

translations.DBH: This is my commandment: that you love one another as I have loved you.
translations.NRSVUE: "This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
translations.LAMSA: This is my commandment: That you love one another just as I have loved you.

translations.DBH: No one has greater love than this: that he should lay down his soul for his friends.
translations.NRSVUE: No one has greater love than this, to lay down one's life for one's friends.
translations.LAMSA: There is no greater love than this, that a man lay down his life for the sake of his friends.

translations.DBH: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
translations.NRSVUE: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
translations.LAMSA: You are my friends if you do everything that I command you.

translations.DBH: I call you slaves no longer, because the slave does not know what his lord is doing; but I have called you friends, because everything I have heard from the Father I have made known to you.
translations.NRSVUE: I do not call you servants any longer, because the servant does not know what the master is doing, but I have called you friends, because I have made known to you everything that I have heard from my Father.
translations.LAMSA: Henceforth I will not call you servants, because a servant does not know what his master does; but I have always called you my friends, because everything that I heard from my Father I have made known to you.

translations.DBH: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you, so that you should go and should bear fruit, and your fruit will last, so that whatever you might ask of the Father in my name he might give you.
translations.NRSVUE: You did not choose me, but I chose you. And I appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, so that the Father will give you whatever you ask him in my name.
translations.LAMSA: You did not choose me, but I chose you, and I have appointed you, that you also should go and produce fruit and that your fruit might remain, so that whatever you ask my Father in my name, he will give it to you.

translations.DBH: These things I command you so that you love one another.
translations.NRSVUE: I am giving you these commands so that you may love one another.
translations.LAMSA: I command these things to you so that you may love one another.

translations.DBH: If the cosmos hates you, you know that it has hated me before you.
translations.NRSVUE: "If the world hates you, be aware that it hated me before it hated you.
translations.LAMSA: If the world hates you, know well that it has hated me before you.

translations.DBH: If you were of the cosmos, the cosmos would have loved its own; but since you are not of the cosmos, for I chose you out of the cosmos, the cosmos therefore hates you.
translations.NRSVUE: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. Because you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.
translations.LAMSA: If you were of the world, the world would love its own; but you are not of the world for I have chosen you out of the world; this is why the world hates you.

translations.DBH: Remember the word that I spoke to you: A slave is not superior to his lord. If they persecuted me they will persecute you as well; if they kept my word they will keep yours as well.
translations.NRSVUE: Remember the word that I said to you, 'Slaves are not greater than their master.' If they persecuted me, they will persecute you; if they kept my word, they will keep yours also.
translations.LAMSA: Remember the word which I said to you, that no servant is greater than his master. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they kept my word, they will also keep yours.

translations.DBH: But all these things they will do to you on account of my name, because they do not know the one who has sent me.
translations.NRSVUE: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they do not know him who sent me.
translations.LAMSA: But they will do all these things to you for the sake of my name, because they do not know him who sent me.

translations.DBH: Had I not come and spoken to them they would have borne no sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
translations.NRSVUE: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have sin, but now they have no excuse for their sin.
translations.LAMSA: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would be without sin; but now they have no excuse for their sins.

translations.DBH: Whoever hates me also hates my Father.
translations.NRSVUE: Whoever hates me hates my Father also.
translations.LAMSA: He who hates me hates my Father also.

translations.DBH: If I had not performed among them the works that no one else had performed, they would have borne no sin; but they have both seen and hated both me and my Father.
translations.NRSVUE: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not have sin. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
translations.LAMSA: If I had not done works before their eyes, such as no other man has ever done, they would be without sin; but now they have seen and hated me and also my Father,

translations.DBH: And thus might the passage written in the Law be fulfilled: 'They hated me without reason.'
translations.NRSVUE: It was to fulfill the word that is written in their law, 'They hated me without a cause.'
translations.LAMSA: So that the word which is written in their law may be fulfilled, They hated me for no reason.

translations.DBH: When the advocate comes, whom I shall send to you from the Father, the Spirit of the truth who comes forth from the Father, he will testify concerning me;
translations.NRSVUE: "When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who comes from the Father, he will testify on my behalf.
translations.LAMSA: But when the Comforter comes, whom I will send to you from my Father, the Spirit of truth which proceeds from my Father, he will testify concerning me.

translations.DBH: and you too must testify, for you are with me from the beginning.
translations.NRSVUE: You also are to testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.
translations.LAMSA: And you also will testify because you have been with me from the beginning.

translations.DBH: "I have spoken these things to you so that you might not be caused to falter.
translations.NRSVUE: "I have said these things to you to keep you from falling away.
translations.LAMSA: I HAVE spoken these things to you so that you may not stumble.

JOH_16_2.reason: Likely narration or spoken by someone else

translations.DBH: They will make you exiles from the synagogue; and an hour is coming in which everyone who kills you thinks he is offering a service to God.
translations.NRSVUE: They will put you out of the synagogues. Indeed, an hour is coming when those who kill you will think that by doing so they are offering worship to God.
translations.LAMSA: For they will put you out of their synagogues; and the hour will come that whoever kills you will think that he has offered an offering to God.

translations.DBH: And they will do these things because they have known neither the Father nor me.
translations.NRSVUE: And they will do this because they have not known the Father or me.
translations.LAMSA: And these things they will do because they have not known my Father, nor me.

translations.DBH: But I have spoken these things so that when their hour arrives you may remember that I told them to you. At first I did not tell you these things, since I was with you.
translations.NRSVUE: But I have said these things to you so that when their hour comes you may remember that I told you about them. "I did not say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
translations.LAMSA: I have spoken these things to you that when their time does come, you may remember them, and that I told you. And these things I did not tell you before because I was with you.

translations.DBH: But now I am going away to the one who has sent me, and not one of you asks me, 'Where are you going?'
translations.NRSVUE: But now I am going to him who sent me, yet none of you asks me, 'Where are you going?'
translations.LAMSA: But now I am going to him who sent me, and yet no one of you asks me, Where are you going?

translations.DBH: But, because I have spoken to you, grief has filled your heart.
translations.NRSVUE: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your hearts.
translations.LAMSA: But because I told you these things, sorrow has come and filled your hearts.

translations.DBH: But I tell you the truth, it is for your own good that I should go away. For if I do not go away the advocate surely is not coming to you; but if I go I shall send him to you.
translations.NRSVUE: Nevertheless, I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you, but if I go, I will send him to you.
translations.LAMSA: But I tell you the truth, It is better for you that I should go away; for if I do not go away, the Comforter will not come to you; but if I should go, I will send him to you.

translations.DBH: And on coming that one will prove the cosmos wrong concerning sin and concerning righteousness and concerning judgment:
translations.NRSVUE: And when he comes, he will prove the world wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment:
translations.LAMSA: And when he is come, he will rebuke the world concerning sin, concerning righteousness, and concerning judgment;

translations.DBH: Concerning sin, because they do not have faith in me;
translations.NRSVUE: about sin, because they do not believe in me;
translations.LAMSA: Concerning sin, because they do not believe in me;

translations.DBH: and concerning righteousness, because I am going to the Father, and you no longer see me;
translations.NRSVUE: about righteousness, because I am going to the Father, and you will see me no longer;
translations.LAMSA: Concerning righteousness, because I go to my Father and you will not see me again;

translations.DBH: and concerning judgment, because the Archon of this cosmos has been judged.
translations.NRSVUE: about judgment, because the ruler of this world has been condemned.
translations.LAMSA: Concerning judgment, because the leader of this world has been judge.

translations.DBH: I still have many things to tell you, but right now you cannot bear them;
translations.NRSVUE: "I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now.
translations.LAMSA: Again, I have many other things to tell you, but you cannot grasp them now.

JOH_16_13.reason: Likely narration or spoken by someone else

translations.DBH: but when that one comes, the Spirit of the truth, he will guide you on the way to all truth; for he will not speak from himself, but will speak what he hears, and he will announce to you things to come.
translations.NRSVUE: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth, for he will not speak on his own but will speak whatever he hears, and he will declare to you the things that are to come.
translations.LAMSA: But when the Spirit of truth is come, he will guide you into all the truth; for he will not speak from himself, but what he hears he will speak; and he will make known to you things which are to come in the future.

translations.DBH: That one will glorify me, because he will receive from what is mine and will announce it to you.
translations.NRSVUE: He will glorify me because he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
translations.LAMSA: He will glorify me, because he will take of my own and show it to you.

translations.DBH: All that the Father has is mine; that is why I said that he receives from what is mine and will announce it to you.
translations.NRSVUE: All that the Father has is mine. For this reason I said that he will take what is mine and declare it to you.
translations.LAMSA: Everything that my Father has is mine; this is the reason why I told you that he will take of my own and show it to you.

translations.DBH: A little while and you see me no longer, and a little while again and you will see me [, because I am going to the Father]."
translations.NRSVUE: "A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me."
translations.LAMSA: A little while, and you will not see me; and again a little while, and you will see me, because I am going to the Father.

JOH_16_17.reason: Likely narration or spoken by someone else

translations.DBH: Therefore some of his disciples said to one another, "What is this that he is telling us: 'A little while and you see me no longer, and a little while again and you will see me'? And: 'Because I am going to the Father'?"
translations.NRSVUE: Then some of his disciples said to one another, "What does he mean by saying to us, 'A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me,' and 'because I am going to the Father'?"
translations.LAMSA: Then his disciples said to one another, What is this that he said to us, A little while, and you will not see me; and again a little while, and you will see me; and, because I am going to my Father?

translations.DBH: So they said, "What is this that he is saying, this 'little while'? We do not know what he is saying."
translations.NRSVUE: They said, "What does he mean by this 'a little while'? We do not know what he is talking about."
translations.LAMSA: And they said, What is this that he said, A little while? We cannot understand what he talks about.

translations.DBH: Jesus knew that they desired to question him and said to them, "Are you asking one another this, because I said, 'A little while and you do not see me, and a little while again and you will see me'?
translations.NRSVUE: Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, so he said to them, "Are you discussing among yourselves what I meant when I said, 'A little while, and you will no longer see me, and again a little while, and you will see me'?
translations.LAMSA: Jesus knew that they desired to ask him, and he said to them, Are you inquiring among yourselves concerning this that I told you, A little while, and you will not see me; and again a little while, and you will see me?

translations.DBH: Amen, amen, I tell you that you will weep and will lament, and the cosmos will rejoice; you will feel anguish but your anguish will become joy.
translations.NRSVUE: Very truly, I tell you, you will weep and mourn, but the world will rejoice; you will have pain, but your pain will turn into joy.
translations.LAMSA: Truly, truly, I say to you that you will weep and wail, and yet the world will rejoice; and you will be sad, but your sadness will be changed into gladness.

JOH_16_21.reason: Likely narration or spoken by someone else

translations.DBH: The woman, when she is giving birth, feels anguish because her hour has come; but, when she produces the child, she no longer remembers the suffering on account of the joy that a human being has been born into the cosmos.
translations.NRSVUE: When a woman is in labor, she has pain because her hour has come. But when her child is born, she no longer remembers the anguish because of the joy of having brought a human being into the world.
translations.LAMSA: When a woman is in travail, she is depressed because her day has arrived; but when she has given birth to a son, she no longer remembers her troubles because of the joy that a male child is born into the world.

translations.DBH: And so now indeed you feel anguish; but I shall see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one takes your joy away from you.
translations.NRSVUE: So you have pain now, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
translations.LAMSA: So you also are depressed; but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and your joy no man will take away from you.

translations.DBH: And on that day you will not ask me any question. Amen, amen, I tell you, whatever you request of the Father in my name he will give you.
translations.NRSVUE: On that day you will ask nothing of me. Very truly, I tell you, if you ask anything of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
translations.LAMSA: In that day you will not ask me anything. Truly, truly, I say to you that whatever you ask my Father in my name, he will give it to you.

translations.DBH: Until now you have not asked anything in my name; ask and you will receive, that your joy might be complete.
translations.NRSVUE: Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.
translations.LAMSA: Hitherto you have asked nothing in my name; ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be full.

translations.DBH: I have spoken these things to you figuratively; an hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures, but will make open proclamation to you concerning the Father.
translations.NRSVUE: "I have said these things to you in figures of speech. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures but will tell you plainly of the Father.
translations.LAMSA: I have spoken these things in figures; but the time is coming when I will not speak to you in figures, but will plainly explain to you concerning the Father.

translations.DBH: On that day you will ask in my name, and I am not saying to you that I will implore the Father on your behalf;
translations.NRSVUE: On that day you will ask in my name. I do not say to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf,
translations.LAMSA: In that day you will ask in my name; and I will not say to you, I will ask the Father concerning you.

translations.DBH: for the Father himself loves you because you have cherished me and have had faith that I come forth from God.
translations.NRSVUE: for the Father himself loves you because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
translations.LAMSA: For the Father himself loves you because you have loved me and have believed that I came forth from the Father.

translations.DBH: I came forth from the Father and have come into the cosmos; I am leaving the cosmos again and am going to the Father."
translations.NRSVUE: I came from the Father and have come into the world; again, I am leaving the world and am going to the Father."
translations.LAMSA: I came forth from the Father and I came into the world; again, I am leaving the world and I am going to the Father.

JOH_16_29.reason: Likely narration or spoken by someone else

translations.DBH: His disciples say, "See, now you speak openly, and utter no figure of speech.
translations.NRSVUE: His disciples said, "Yes, now you are speaking plainly, not in any figure of speech!
translations.LAMSA: His disciples said to him, Behold, now you speak plainly and do not utter a single figure.

translations.DBH: Now we know that you know all things and have no need that anyone should question you. By this we have faith that you came forth from God."
translations.NRSVUE: Now we know that you know all things and do not need to have anyone question you; by this we believe that you came from God."
translations.LAMSA: Now we understand that you know everything, and you need no man to ask you; by this we believe that you have come forth from God.

translations.DBH: Jesus answered them, "Now you have faith?
translations.NRSVUE: Jesus answered them, "Do you now believe?
translations.LAMSA: Jesus said to them, Believe;

translations.DBH: See: An hour is coming--has indeed come--when you are scattered, each one to his own home, and you leave me alone; and I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
translations.NRSVUE: The hour is coming, indeed it has come, when you will be scattered, each one to his home, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone because the Father is with me.
translations.LAMSA: For behold, the hour is coming, and it has now come, when you will be dispersed, every man to his own country, and you will leave me alone; and yet I am never alone because the Father is with me.

translations.DBH: I have spoken these things to you so that you might have peace in me. In the cosmos you have suffering; but take heart--I have conquered the cosmos."

translations.DBH: I pray not that you should take them out of the cosmos, but that you should keep them away from the wicked one.

translations.DBH: They are not of the cosmos, just as I am not of the cosmos.

translations.LAMSA: He said to him again the third time, Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me? It grieved Peter because he said to him the third time, Do you love me? So he said to him, My Lord, you understand well everything, you know that I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my ewes.

translations.DBH: Therefore, write down the things you have seen and the things that are and the things about to occur thereafter.
translations.LAMSA: Write, therefore, the things which you have seen and the things which are and the things which shall be hereafter,

translations.DBH: The mystery of the seven stars you saw upon my right hand and the seven gold lampstands--the seven stars are angels of the seven assemblies, and the seven lampstands are seven assemblies.
translations.LAMSA: The mystery of the seven stars which you saw in my right hand, and the seven golden candlesticks. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches and the seven candlesticks are the seven churches.

translations.DBH: "Write this to the angel of the assembly in Ephesus: 'The one who wields the seven stars in his right hand, who walks amid the seven golden lampstands, says this:
translations.LAMSA: TO the angel of the church of Ephesus write: These things says the Omnipotent One who holds the seven stars in his right hand, who walks in the midst of the seven golden candlesticks.

translations.DBH: "I know your works, and your labor and endurance, and that you cannot bear evil men, and have tested those who claim to be apostles and are no such thing, and have found them to be false,
translations.LAMSA: I know your works and your labour and your patience and how you cannot endure those who are ungodly; you have tried those who say they are apostles and are not, and you have found them liars;

translations.DBH: and you have endurance, and have borne up because of my name, and have not grown weary.
translations.LAMSA: And you have patience, and have borne burdens for my name's sake, and have not wearied.

translations.DBH: But I hold it against you that you abandoned your first love.
translations.LAMSA: Nevertheless I have something against you, because you have left your first love.

translations.DBH: Therefore remember from where you have fallen, and change your heart, and practice the works you practiced at first; and, if not, I am coming to you, and will remove your lamp- stand from its place--unless you change your heart.
translations.LAMSA: Remember therefore from whence you have fallen and repent and do the first works; or else I will come to you very soon, and I will remove your candlestick from its place unless you repent.

translations.DBH: But this you do have: that you hate the works of the Nicolaitians, which I hate as well.
translations.LAMSA: But this you have in your favour, you hate the works of the Nicolaitanes, which I also hate.

translations.DBH: Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To the one who conquers, I shall grant it to him to eat from the tree of life, which is in God's paradise."'
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches: To him who overcomes, I will give to eat of the tree of life, which is in the midst of the paradise of my God.

translations.DBH: "And to the angel of the assembly in Smyrna write: 'The first and the last, who became dead and who lived, says these things:
translations.LAMSA: And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write: These things says the first and the last, which was dead and is alive:

translations.DBH: "I know [your works and] your affliction and destitution, yet you are rich, and the blasphemy of those who call themselves Judaeans and are not, but who are instead a synagogue of the Accuser.
translations.LAMSA: I know your works and your suffering and poverty, but you are rich, and I know the blasphemy of those who say they are Jews and are not, but are of the synagogue of Satan.

translations.DBH: Do not fear the things you are about to suffer. See: the Slanderer is about to cast some of you into prison so that you may be tested, and you will suffer affliction for ten days. Be faithful unto death and I will grant you the chapletd of life.
translations.LAMSA: Fear none of those things which you shall suffer; behold, the devil will cast some of you into prison, that you be tried; and you will be oppressed for ten days. Be faithful even to death, and I will give you a crown of life.

translations.DBH: Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. The one who conquers will by no means be harmed by the second death."'
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches: He who overcomes shall not be hurt by the second death.

translations.DBH: "And to the angel of the assembly in Pergamum write: 'The one who has the sharp two-edged sword says these things:
translations.LAMSA: And to the angel of the church in Pergamos write: These things says he who has the sharp two-edged sword:

translations.DBH: "I know [your works and] where you dwell--there where the throne of the Accuser is-- and that you hold fast to my name, and did not renounce my faith even in the days of Antipas, my witness, my faithful one, who was killed right by your side, there where the Accuser dwells.
translations.LAMSA: I know your works and where you dwell, even where Satan's seat is; and you uphold my name, and you did not deny my faith, even in those days when that witness of mine appeared, that faithful one of mine who was slain among you, where Satan dwells.

translations.DBH: But I hold a few things against you, because you have some persons there who hold onto the teachings of Balaam, who taught Balak to throw down a snare before the sons of Israel, to eat sacrifices offered to idols, and to engage in whoring.
translations.LAMSA: But I have a few things against you because you have there those who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to cast a stumbling block before the children of Israel, to eat things sacrificed to idols and to commit adultery.

translations.DBH: nevertheless, hold onto what you have until I shall come.
translations.LAMSA: But hold fast to that which you already have till I come.

translations.DBH: And the one who conquers, and who abides by my works until the end, to him I will give power over the gentiles--
translations.LAMSA: And he who overcomes and keeps my works until the end, to him I will give authority over the nations;

translations.DBH: 'And he will shepherd them with a rod of iron: they are shattered like clay vessels'--28just as I also received it from my Father; and I will give him the Morning Star.
translations.LAMSA: And he shall shepherd them with a rod of iron; like the vessels of the potter, they shall be shattered, even as I was disciplined by my Father.
And I will give him the morning star.

translations.DBH: Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies."'
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

translations.DBH: "And to the angel of the assembly in Sardis write: 'The one who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars says these things: "I know your works--that you have won a name for being alive, yet you are dead.
translations.LAMSA: AND to the angel of the church in Sardis write: These things says he who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars: I know your works; you have a name that you are alive and yet you are dead.

translations.DBH: Be vigilant, fortify the things that remain that are moribund, for I have not found your works to be fulfilled in the eyes of my God;
translations.LAMSA: Awake, and hold fast to the things which remain but are ready to die; for I have not found your works perfect before my God.

translations.DBH: remember, therefore, how you have received and listened, and keep watch and change your hearts. Should you not be vigilant, then, I shall come like a thief, and you surely would not know at what hour I shall come upon you.
translations.LAMSA: Remember, therefore, just as you have received and heard, so hold fast and repent. And if, therefore, you do not awake, I will come against you as a thief, and you shall not know at what hour I will come upon you.

translations.DBH: But you have in Sardis a few who can be named who did not sully their garments, and they shall walk with me in whites, because they are worthy.
translations.LAMSA: But you have a few members at Sardis who have not defiled their names; and they shall walk with me in white, for they are worthy.

translations.DBH: The one who conquers shall thus be wrapped in white garments, and I most certainly will not expunge his name from the book of life, and I will acknowledge his name before my Father and before his angels.
translations.LAMSA: He who overcomes, the same shall be clothed in white robes; and I will not blot his name out of the book of life, but I will confess his name before my Father and before his angels.

translations.DBH: Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies."'
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

translations.DBH: "And to the angel of the assembly in Philadelphia write: 'The holy one, the true one, who holds the key of David, the one who opens--and none shall close--and who closes--and none opens--says these things:
translations.LAMSA: And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write: These things says he who is the holy one, he who is true, he who has the key of David, he who opens and no man shuts, and shuts, and no man opens;

translations.DBH: "I know your works. See: I have provided an opened door before you, one that none can close, for you have small power, yet you have kept my word and not denied my name.
translations.LAMSA: I know your works and behold, I have set before you an open door which no man can lock, for you have but little strength and yet you have obeyed my word and have not denied my name.

translations.DBH: See: I make a gift of some from the synagogue of the Accuser--those who say they are Judaeans, yet are not, but are lying. Look: I will make it so that they shall come and prostrate themselves before your feet, and they shall know that I have loved you.
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I turn over those of the synagogue of Satan, who say that they are Jews and are not, but do lie; behold, I will make them to come and worship before your feet, and to know that I have loved you.

translations.DBH: Because you have kept my instruction to endure, I will also keep you from the hour of the trial that is about to come upon the whole inhabited world, to test those who dwell upon the earth.
translations.LAMSA: Because you have kept the word of my patience, I also will keep you from the hour of temptation which shall come upon all the world to try those who dwell upon earth.

translations.DBH: I am coming very quickly; hold fast to what you have so that no one take your chaplet.
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I come quickly. Hold that fast which you have, so that no man take your crown.

translations.DBH: The one who conquers, I will make him a pillar in the sanctuary of my God, and he shall surely not go out of it any longer, and I will inscribe upon him my God's name and the name of my God's city--the New Jerusalem descending out of heaven from my God--as well as my new name.
translations.LAMSA: He who overcomes I will make a pillar in the temple of my God, and he shall not go out again; and I will write upon him the name of my God and the name of the new Jerusalem which comes down out of heaven from my God; and I will write upon him my new name.

translations.DBH: Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies."'
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

translations.DBH: "And to the angel of the assembly in Laodicea write: 'The Amen, the witness faithful and true, the origing of God's creation, says these things:
translations.LAMSA: And to the angel of the church in Laodicea write: These things says the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God;

translations.DBH: "I know your works--that you are neither cold nor hot. I would that you were cold or hot.
translations.LAMSA: I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot; it is better to be either cold or hot.

translations.DBH: Thus, since you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I am about to vomit you from my mouth.
translations.LAMSA: So then because you are lukewarm, and neither cold nor hot, I will spue you out of my mouth.

translations.DBH: For you say, 'I am rich, and have been enriched, and need nothing,' and you do not realize that you are someone wretched and pitiable and destitute and blind and naked.
translations.LAMSA: You say, I am rich and my wealth has increased and I need nothing; and you do not know that you are miserable and a wanderer and poor and blind and naked.

translations.DBH: I counsel you to buy gold refined by fire from me so that you may be rich, and white garments so that you may be clad and the shame of your nakedness may not be made manifest, and salve to anoint your eyes so that you may see.
translations.LAMSA: I advise you to buy of me gold refined in the fire, that you may become rich; and white raiment, that you may be clothed, so that the shame of your nakedness may not be seen; and anoint your eyes with salve, that you may see.

translations.DBH: As many as I love I rebuke and discipline; so be zealous and change your heart.
translations.LAMSA: I rebuke and chastise all those whom I love; be zealous, therefore, and repent.

translations.DBH: Look: I stand at the door and knock; should anyone hear my voice and open the door, I will enter in to him, and I will dine with him and he with me.
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I stand at the door and knock; if any man hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to him and will sup with him, and he with me.

translations.DBH: The one who conquers, I will grant it to him to sit with me upon my throne, just as I also conquered and sat with my Father on his throne.
translations.LAMSA: To him who overcomes I will grant to sit with me on my throne, even as I also overcame and have sat down with my Father on his throne.

translations.DBH: Whoever has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies."'"
translations.LAMSA: He who has ears, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.

translations.DBH: "Look: I am coming like a thief; how blissful the one who remains vigilant and keeps his clothes on, lest he walk naked and they see his shame."
translations.LAMSA: And behold, I come as a thief. Blessed is he who watches and keeps his garments, lest he must walk naked and they see his shame.

translations.DBH: "And look: I am coming quickly. How blissful the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book."
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I am coming soon; blessed is he who keeps the sayings of the prophecy of this book.

translations.DBH: "Look: I am coming quickly, and with me is the recompense I have to render to each, according to what his work is.ba
translations.LAMSA: Behold, I am coming soon and my reward is with me, to give every man according as his work shall be.

translations.DBH: I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end."
translations.LAMSA: I am Aleph and Tau, the beginning and the end, the first and the last.

REV_22_14.reason: Likely narration or spoken by someone else

translations.DBH: How blissful are those who wash their robes,bb so that they shall be given sanction for the tree of life, and may enter by the gates into the city.
translations.LAMSA: Blessed are those who do his commandments, that they may have the right to the tree of life, and may enter in through the gates into the city.

REV_22_15.reason: Likely narration or spoken by someone else

translations.DBH: Outside are the curs and the sorcerers and the whorish and the murderers and the idolaters and everyone who loves and practices falsehood.
translations.LAMSA: For without are the vicious and magicians and the immoral and murderers and idolaters and whoever loves to tell lies.

translations.DBH: "I Jesus sent my angel to attest these things to you for the assemblies. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright Morning Star."bc
translations.LAMSA: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to testify to you these things in the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright morning star.

translations.DBH: The one who attests these things says, "Yes, I am coming quickly." Amen, [yes,] come, Lord Jesus.
translations.LAMSA: He who testifies these things says, Surely I am coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus.
